title: Additive pipeline plumbing + medical team registration
description: Risk-first integration linchpin. Add `medical-sop` as a new additive PipelineEngineName member (src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts) and widen every Zod enum that mirrors it (team pipelineShape at src/config/schema.ts:366 + the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum) in lockstep. Extend BOTH exhaustive switches in src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts: selectPipelineEngineForTeam returns a new MedicalSopEngine for a `medical-sop` team; selectPipelineEngine (the config/programming path that reads config.pipeline.engine and can never legitimately be medical-sop) handles the member defensively (fall through to TsPipelineEngine) so the switch stays exhaustive without changing programming behavior. Create a new src/medical/ module with a MINIMAL MedicalSopEngine stub (implements PipelineEngine; run returns a trivial PipelineResult for now), the GuardrailSet type filling Team.guardrails, and buildMedicalTeam(config) + registry wiring so loadTeam(config, 'medical') resolves to a Team with pipelineShape 'medical-sop'. Byte-zero impact on ts|skill|workflow and the programming team is a HARD requirement. This sprint is plumbing + skeleton only — gates, store, numerics, ingestion, egress, and retrieval are later sprints.

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass with zero failures, including new collocated tests under src/medical (npm run test), and the FULL pre-existing suite remains green (no regression).

PipelineEngineName (src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts) includes 'medical-sop' and every Zod enum mirroring it is widened in lockstep: the team pipelineShape enum (src/config/schema.ts:366) and the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum. A unit test parses a config/team declaring pipelineShape 'medical-sop' successfully and asserts the TS union and the Zod enum agree.

Both switches in src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts are exhaustive over the widened PipelineEngineName with the compile-time `never` guard intact. selectPipelineEngineForTeam(medicalTeam, config) returns a MedicalSopEngine instance (instanceof / name === 'medical-sop'); selectPipelineEngine(config) is unchanged for ts/skill/workflow.

loadTeam(config, 'medical') returns a Team with id 'medical', pipelineShape === 'medical-sop', and a concrete GuardrailSet object in its guardrails slot (not undefined). A unit test asserts these fields.

Regression: loadTeam(config) with no id still returns the byte-identical programming team (providers deep-equal resolveRoleProviders(config), pipelineShape === resolveEngineName(config), memoryNamespace unchanged); a test asserts the programming team and existing engine selection are unchanged.
No @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai SDK import appears anywhere under src/medical (grep returns nothing); the module is ESM/NodeNext with .js import extensions and strict TS.

nonGoals: Do not implement the consent gate, audit log, or disclaimer — that is Sprint 2 (the MedicalSopEngine stub may have placeholder hooks but no enforcement).
Do not implement the red-flag detector / GuardrailSet.evaluate logic — that is Sprint 3 (define the GuardrailSet TYPE only this sprint).
Do not implement HealthDataStore or NumericsQueryLayer — that is Sprint 4.
Do not implement ingestion or the CLI import command — that is Sprint 5.
Do not implement EgressGuard, the ESLint medical boundary, medications wiring, or the full SOP — that is Sprint 6.
Do not implement LiteratureRetriever networking — that is Sprint 7.
Do not change the behavior of ts|skill|workflow engines, selectPipelineEngine for those names, resolveRoleProviders, runPipeline, or the programming team.
stopConditions: medical-sop is wired through PipelineEngineName + both Zod enums + both selector switches with the never guard intact, loadTeam(config,'medical') resolves to a medical-sop team with a GuardrailSet, and the programming team + ts/skill/workflow are byte-identical
definitionOfDone: A new src/medical/ module exposes a minimal MedicalSopEngine (implements PipelineEngine; readonly name = 'medical-sop'; run returns a trivial PipelineResult), a GuardrailSet type, and buildMedicalTeam(config): Team. PipelineEngineName gains the additive 'medical-sop' member; the team pipelineShape Zod enum (schema.ts:366) and the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum are widened to match. Both selector switches handle medical-sop exhaustively (team switch -> MedicalSopEngine; config switch -> defensive TsPipelineEngine fall-through). loadTeam registers a built-in 'medical' team. Build, typecheck, and the full test suite pass; the programming team and ts|skill|workflow behavior are byte-identical; no SDK types leak into src/medical.
assumptions: PipelineEngine is an INTERFACE (src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:9) — MedicalSopEngine implements it (the architecture's `extends` is shorthand). run signature: run(userPrompt, projectRoot, config, opts?: { runId?: string }): Promise<PipelineResult>.
resolveEngineNameForTeam (selector.ts:75) returns team.pipelineShape verbatim for any value except 'workflow' (only workflow is downgraded), so 'medical-sop' flows through unchanged — verified.

selectPipelineEngine (selector.ts:51) reads config.pipeline.engine and will never legitimately be 'medical-sop'; the added case is a defensive exhaustiveness branch (fall through to TsPipelineEngine) and changes no real programming path.
Team.guardrails is `?: unknown` today (types.ts:29); a concrete GuardrailSet type is assigned only for the medical team and leaves other teams' undefined guardrails valid.
The 'medical' team is registered in code in src/teams/registry.ts (like the built-in 'programming' team), not via a .bober/teams/*.json file.
outOfScope: Any change to existing engine/team public behavior
generatorNotes:
Create src/medical/ with small single-purpose files + unicode box-header section comments (project principles). Suggested files: src/medical/types.ts (GuardrailSet, GuardrailVerdict, GuardrailContext, MedicalAnswer, and shared medical types), src/medical/engine.ts (MedicalSopEngine stub), src/medical/team.ts (buildMedicalTeam), with collocated *.test.ts.

VERIFIED integration anchors (read these before editing):
- src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:6  export type PipelineEngineName = 'ts' | 'skill' | 'workflow';  -> add '| "medical-sop"'. The doc comment says it 'Mirrors the z.enum in PipelineSectionSchema' — find and widen that enum too.
- src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:9  export interface PipelineEngine { readonly name: PipelineEngineName; run(userPrompt, projectRoot, config, opts?: {runId?: string}): Promise<PipelineResult>; }  -> MedicalSopEngine implements this.
- src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:51 selectPipelineEngine(config) switch (resolveEngineName(config)) — has cases ts/skill/workflow and NO default; adding the union member breaks exhaustiveness, so ADD `case 'medical-sop': return new TsPipelineEngine();` (defensive — config.pipeline.engine is never medical-sop) OR a `never`-typed default; keep ts/skill/workflow byte-identical.
- src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:103 selectPipelineEngineForTeam(team, config) switch (resolveEngineNameForTeam(team, config)) — ADD `case 'medical-sop': return new MedicalSopEngine();` (import from ../../medical/engine.js).
- src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:75 resolveEngineNameForTeam returns `requested` verbatim unless 'workflow' — medical-sop passes through; no change needed there.
- src/config/schema.ts:366 pipelineShape: z.enum(['ts','skill','workflow']).optional() -> add 'medical-sop'. Also widen the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum (search schema.ts for the other z.enum(['ts','skill','workflow'])).
- src/teams/types.ts:27,29 pipelineShape: PipelineEngineName; guardrails?: unknown.
- src/teams/registry.ts:34 loadTeam(config, teamId?) — register a built-in 'medical' team. Mirror how the 'programming' team is built. buildMedicalTeam(config) returns { id:'medical', displayName:'Medical team', memoryNamespace:'medical', providers: resolveRoleProviders(config), pipelineShape:'medical-sop', roles: <descriptors>, guardrails: <a concrete GuardrailSet instance with rulesetVersion> }.

MedicalSopEngine stub: readonly name = 'medical-sop' as const; async run(...) returns a minimal PipelineResult (look at PipelineResult shape at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:66 and what TsPipelineEngine.run returns for the required fields — return a stub success/placeholder result; the real SOP lands in S2/S3/S6). Do NOT call any LLM in the stub.

GuardrailSet TYPE this sprint: define `interface GuardrailSet { evaluate(prompt: string, ctx: GuardrailContext): GuardrailVerdict; readonly rulesetVersion: string }` and a trivial implementation that returns { kind: 'allow' } (real logic in S3) so buildMedicalTeam can fill the slot. GuardrailVerdict union per the data model.

Keep createDefaultConfig emitting NO teams (back-compat). Tests: loadTeam(config,'medical') field assertions; selectPipelineEngineForTeam(medicalTeam) instanceof MedicalSopEngine; programming-team regression (deep-equal providers + engine unchanged); Zod parse of pipelineShape:'medical-sop'. Use no network, no fs (pure).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-1-4: grep engine.ts for the widened union; parse via the team schema a config with a team whose pipelineShape is 'medical-sop' (expect success) and one with 'bogus' (expect Zod failure); confirm the PipelineSectionSchema engine enum was also widened (search for the second z.enum).
sc-1-5: assert both switches compile with the never guard (typecheck) and that removing a case would fail; runtime: selectPipelineEngineForTeam(buildMedicalTeam(config), config).name === 'medical-sop'; selectPipelineEngine(config) for engine 'ts'/'workflow' returns the same engine type as before.
sc-1-6: loadTeam(config,'medical') -> id==='medical', pipelineShape==='medical-sop', guardrails is a non-undefined object exposing rulesetVersion + evaluate.
sc-1-7: REGRESSION — loadTeam(config) (no id) deep-equals the programming team (providers === resolveRoleProviders(config), pipelineShape === resolveEngineName(config)); run the FULL suite and confirm nothing else changed.
sc-1-8: grep -r '@anthropic-ai/sdk\|from "openai"' src/medical — expect none.
Build + typecheck must be clean. Confirm the MedicalSopEngine stub makes zero LLM calls (no provider import used in engine.ts beyond types).
